title: tetrapterys calophylla subsp. glabrifolia
intro: Tetrapterys calophylla subsp. glabrifolia, also known as the Glabrous Tetrapterys, is a plant species belonging to the family Malpighiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Tetrapterys calophylla subsp. glabrifolia include reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used to make a poultice or ointment to apply topically to affected areas.
